meta_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve Stuck Projects (Checklist)
content.seo_description: Break through the plateau of basic apps. Learn to use Git, Antigravity, and Claude Code to master version control and ship professional-grade software.
content.answer_block: You are stuck because your development workflow lacks professional version control and intelligent feedback loops. By integrating Git with AI-driven agents like Antigravity and Claude Code, you transform your codebase into a managed, self-documenting project, allowing you to debug complex logic, automate commits, and ship production-ready applications with confidence.
content.intro:
The screen glows, the cursor blinks, and your code feels like a house of cards. You’ve mastered the 'Hello World' tutorials, but the moment you try to build something that actually solves a problem, you hit a wall of cryptic errors and messy commits. It is the classic 'Tutorial Purgatory'—where progress stalls and motivation fades.

But the gap between a student project and a professional SaaS isn't just code; it's the workflow. By mastering Git as your safety net and AI as your pair programmer, you stop fighting your tools and start engineering solutions. This is your roadmap to professional-grade development.

steps.title: Initialize with Intent
steps.description: Stop just creating folders. Use Git to track your learning journey from the first file creation, ensuring you can revert to a 'known-good' state anytime.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a professional repository structure for a web app. Include a .gitignore for Node.js, a README.md template, and a clear folder hierarchy for src, tests, and docs.

steps.title: Semantic Commit Automation
steps.description: Stop writing 'fixed stuff' in your commits. Use AI to generate descriptive, conventional commits that tell a story of your progress for future employers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current staged changes and generate a conventional commit message that follows the pattern: <type>(<scope>): <short description>.

steps.title: Automated Code Review
steps.description: Before pushing, let an AI agent audit your logic for common anti-patterns that keep student projects from reaching production quality.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the code in the current branch for security vulnerabilities, performance bottlenecks, and readability issues. Suggest 3 specific improvements.

steps.title: Branching for Feature Growth
steps.description: Learn to isolate features. Never build directly on main; use branching to experiment with risky features without breaking your stable base.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a new branch strategy for this project that separates core logic from experimental UI components. Explain the merge flow.

steps.title: Context-Aware Documentation
steps.description: Bridge the gap between code and documentation. Use AI to maintain your README as your project evolves, so it remains a portfolio-ready asset.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Update the README.md based on the latest changes in the src folder. Include a section on how to run the project and the technical stack used.

pro_tips: Treat your Git history as your technical resume; employers look at your commit frequency and quality, not just the final product.
Use 'Git bisect' when you break a feature; AI can help you identify exactly which commit introduced the regression.
Always keep your AI context small; don't dump your whole project into the prompt, feed it specific modules to get higher quality results.
Automate your testing pipeline early; even simple Jest tests will save you hours of debugging when you scale.
Use AI to explain legacy code you've written; if you can't understand your own code from a month ago, it's time to refactor.
content.next_steps: After completing this, deploy your project to Vercel or Netlify and add the link to your GitHub profile. Start your next feature branch, and try to build it using only AI-assisted Git workflows.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the strategy, and the roadmap. The only thing missing is the commitment to ship. Don't let your ideas stay in your local environment. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
